audit_items.proof_trace: `ld == NULL` is explicitly guarded at L35 before `ld->ld_debug` is accessed. The function pointer `ber_pvt_log_print` is initialized to `ber_error_print` (non-NULL) at `bprint.c:90`, and the only setter in `options.c:144` is gated by a NULL check (L131-135) that returns an error if `invalue == NULL`, so the pointer cannot be nulled. The `fmt` parameter has no runtime NULL guard, but all callers pass compile-time string literals via the Debug0/1/2/3 macros and there is no externally-controlled path into `ldap_log_printf` with an attacker-supplied `fmt`.
